name: Slack
one_liner: Team messaging and collaboration platform with an extensive HTTP API for building bots, apps, and agent integrations across channels and workspaces.
access_notes: Anyone can create a Slack app for free at api.slack.com/apps, install it into a (free-tier) workspace via OAuth 2.0 v2, and obtain working bot/user tokens with no payment or human approval. Some admin/enterprise methods require paid/Enterprise Grid plans, but standard messaging APIs work on free workspaces.
api_notes: Web API is HTTP JSON RPC-style with 200+ methods across families (chat, conversations, users, files, views, admin, etc.). Also Events API (HTTP subscriptions), Socket Mode / RTM (WebSocket), incoming/outgoing webhooks, and official SDKs (Bolt) for JS/Python/Java.
mcp_notes: Slack (Salesforce) ships an official hosted MCP server at mcp.slack.com, documented at docs.slack.dev/ai/slack-mcp-server, letting MCP clients like Claude search channels, send messages, and take Slack actions.
main_blocker: None for core messaging; a few admin/Enterprise Grid endpoints require paid enterprise plans, and OAuth app setup plus scopes/workspace install is a modest onboarding step.

evidence.claim: Slack uses OAuth 2.0 v2 issuing bot and user tokens; no API key auth documented.
Slack Web API exposes a large set of methods across many families (chat, conversations, users, admin, files, etc.).
Slack ships an official MCP server for AI clients to search and act in Slack.

open_questions: Exact split of which methods require paid Enterprise Grid vs free workspaces; whether the official MCP server needs a paid Slack plan for full functionality.
